summary: Backend fully passing (5/5 pytest). Frontend: Landing, Setup, L1 questions 1-14, skip counter all OK. CRITICAL bug on Q15: Level1Question crashes with `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'prompt')` blocking the l1-complete screen and rest of the journey (L2/Insights/L3 cannot be reached via UI).

ui_bugs.issue: Crashes after the 15th question with TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'prompt'). When Player B answers Q15 the reducer sets questionIndex=15, phase='l1-both-final'. Component re-renders (race with phase switch / strict-mode effect / timer tick) and accesses LEVEL_1_QUESTIONS[15] which is undefined, then dereferences q.prompt, q.a, q.b. This pops the dev error overlay and prevents the user from ever reaching l1-complete-screen, l1-decline, l2-*, insights, l3-teaser.

integration_issues.issue: Entire post-Q15 flow is unreachable through the UI due to the crash above. Could not verify l1-complete-screen, l1-continue-btn, l1-decline-screen, l1-accept-btn, l2-dice-roll-btn, l2-category-continue-btn, l2-card-pick-btn, l2-answer-input (120 char cap), l2-submit-btn, l2-handoff-ready-btn, l2-roll-again-btn, l2-insights-btn, insights-grid (6 cards), insights-l3-btn, insights-restart-btn, l3-teaser-card.

action_items: Fix Level1Question crash at Q15: guard `q = LEVEL_1_QUESTIONS[questionIndex]` with `if (!q) return null;` BEFORE rendering and BEFORE the timer effect dispatches another L1_ANSWER. Also stop the timer effect when questionIndex >= LEVEL_1_QUESTIONS.length (currently the effect can fire `dispatch({type:'L1_ANSWER', choice:'timeout'})` even when no question exists).
Optional hardening: in gameStore L1_ANSWER reducer, no-op if questionIndex is already past last question to avoid double-dispatch.
After fix, re-run e2e to verify l1-complete -> l2 -> insights -> l3 path.
critical_code_review_comments: Level1Question.jsx line 14: `const q = LEVEL_1_QUESTIONS[questionIndex]` has no bounds check. Both render (q.prompt, q.a, q.b) and timer effect will throw on out-of-range index.
Level1Question.jsx timer useEffect dispatches L1_ANSWER on seconds<=0 without checking if component is still on a valid question — can cause spurious dispatches during phase transitions.
server.py /api/sessions/{id} returns `None` (HTTP 200 with body null) for missing sessions instead of 404; minor REST hygiene issue.
server.py uses deprecated @app.on_event('shutdown'); should migrate to lifespan handler.
seed_data_creation: Created test session via POST /api/sessions during backend test (player_a_name='TEST_Alice', player_b_name='TEST_Bob') — not cleaned up; harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is 100% green — re-running pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is sufficient. For frontend, after the Q15 fix, re-run the playwright loop: pick option A 15 times with handoff in between, then verify l1-complete-screen appears, continue -> accept -> dice roll -> category continue -> card pick -> type answer (test 120-char cap) -> submit -> handoff -> Player B answer -> insights -> 6 cards -> peek L3.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: Level1Question.jsx unconditionally reads LEVEL_1_QUESTIONS[questionIndex] and its `.prompt/.a/.b` properties. After Player B answers the 15th question (questionIndex=14), the reducer (gameStore.js L1_ANSWER branch, lines 79-92) increments questionIndex to 15 and sets phase to 'l1-both-final' in the SAME state update. Game.jsx should then mount Level1BothAnswered. However, React renders the new state with the still-mounted Level1Question component once (during the commit/effect phase, the timer useEffect can also fire) resulting in q===undefined and a TypeError. Reproduction: complete 15 L1 questions clicking option A each turn with handoff between -> dev error overlay appears, blocks UI. Mitigation: add `if (!q) return null;` after line 14 in Level1Question.jsx and guard the timer effect with `if (questionIndex >= LEVEL_1_QUESTIONS.length) return;`.
